channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts’ Recent Decisions Raise Concerns Among Voting Rights Experts
item.description: In recent weeks, Chief Justice John Roberts has made rapid decisions that have surprised legal experts, particularly in the field of voting rights. Professor Richard Hasen, a leading scholar from the ...

item.title: Prince Harry speaks out against rising antisemitism and prejudice in the UK
item.description: Prince Harry, Duke of Sussex, has expressed concern over a troubling rise in antisemitism across the United Kingdom, linking it to violent incidents against Jewish communities. Writing in the New Stat...

item.title: Moderate Daily Coffee or Tea Intake Linked to Lower Dementia Risk
item.description: A large long-term study tracking over 131,000 healthcare professionals for up to 43 years suggests that moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee or tea may significantly reduce the risk of dementia....

item.title: US President Trump meets Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing for strategic talks
item.description: US President Donald Trump held a significant meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping at Beijing's Great Hall of the People, marking a crucial moment in international diplomacy. The summit included h...

item.title: UAE Refutes Claims of Netanyahu's Alleged Secret Visit
item.description: The United Arab Emirates on Thursday officially rejected claims that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made a secret visit to the country. Netanyahu’s office had earlier announced that he met ...

item.title: Health risks for England supporters due to extreme Texas heat at World Cup matches
item.description: England supporters attending the World Cup opener against Croatia in Texas face significant health risks from extreme heat, according to academic research. Although AT&T Stadium in Arlington is air-co...

item.title: Tanzania Sees Steady Growth in Mortgage Market Amid Housing Demand
item.description: The Tanzanian mortgage market experienced moderate growth in the third quarter of 2025, reaching TZS 720.81 billion, up from TZS 694.35 billion in Q2. Compared to the same period last year, the market...

item.title: George and Amal Clooney seen at Poznan airport ahead of flight from Poland
item.description: George Clooney and his wife Amal were spotted at Poznan airport in Poland as they prepared to board a private jet on Wednesday. The 65-year-old Hollywood actor looked effortlessly stylish in a black s...

item.title: CF Montreal and Portland Timbers finish in a 2-2 tie in MLS match
item.description: In a closely contested Major League Soccer match, CF Montreal and the Portland Timbers played to a 2-2 draw. Both teams demonstrated strong attacking strategies and solid defensive efforts, keeping fa...

item.title: Assam CM lauds West Bengal decision to allocate land for India-Bangladesh border fencing
item.description: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma expressed his strong approval of the West Bengal Cabinet’s recent decision to allocate land to the Border Security Force (BSF) for the erection of fencing alon...

item.title: DCI nab two suspects in Mlolongo and Westlands land fraud cases
item.description: Nairobi detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations' Land Fraud Investigations Unit have apprehended two individuals linked to separate land fraud cases in Mlolongo and Westlands, Nairo...

item.title: How 24 Rabbits Sparked a Continent-Wide Ecological Crisis in Australia
item.description: In 1859, Thomas Austin, an English settler in Victoria, Australia, released 13–24 European rabbits on his estate for hunting purposes. Although rabbits had arrived in small numbers earlier, this intro...

item.title: Water Discolouration and Outages Affect Gungahlin Residents Following Valve Issue
item.description: Residents of Gungahlin and surrounding suburbs, including Forde, Harrison, Franklin, Palmerston, Nicholls and Ngunnawal, experienced brown tap water and water outages due to a valve problem on the loc...

item.title: Court postpones Medicare24 case to verify Masemola’s finances and evidence from Matlala
item.description: The Pretoria Magistrate’s Court has approved the state’s request to postpone proceedings in the R360 million Medicare24 corruption case to 26 June to allow further investigation. The case involves sen...

item.title: ESPN reportedly opposes expanding the College Football Playoff beyond 16 teams despite conference support
item.description: The College Football Playoff (CFP) is at the center of a debate over potential expansion. After moving from a four-team to a 12-team format, discussions have emerged about further growth. Major confer...

item.title: Life of the O’Hare Family: Liverpool’s Postwar Household with 16 Children
item.description: The O’Hare family of Liverpool gained attention in 1949–1950 as one of Britain’s largest postwar families. Henry O’Hare, an upholsterer, and his wife Marjorie lived in a four-story house in Anfield an...

item.title: Remembering Luciana Paluzzi, Iconic Actress and Bond Villain
item.description: Luciana Paluzzi, born in Rome, Italy, is best remembered for her striking role as Fiona Volpe in the 1965 James Bond classic, Thunderball. Originally auditioning for the lead role of Domino, Paluzzi i...

item.title: Russia escalates daytime drone attacks on Ukraine amid ongoing conflict and diplomatic efforts
item.description: On 13 May 2026, Russia launched a significant daytime drone assault on Ukraine, deploying over 200 Shahed-type drones. Air raid alerts were sounded across multiple regions, including Kyiv, with explos...

item.title: Brett Ratner joins Trump’s official delegation to China, drawing criticism
item.description: During President Donald Trump's diplomatic trip to China on May 14, 2026, Brett Ratner, a film director known for the 'Melania' documentary on Amazon, unexpectedly accompanied the official U.S. delega...

item.title: Senator Bato Dela Rosa Leaves Senate Premises After Gunfire Incident
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa, previously implicated by the International Criminal Court as a suspect in alleged human rights violations during former President Rodrigo Duterte’s anti-drug campaign,...
